title: Battle of the Heroes
content:
{{Top|music}} {{Music |image=[[File:Battle_of_the_heroes.png]] |title="Battle of the Heroes" |composer=[[John Williams]][[Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith (soundtrack)|''Revenge of the Sith'' soundtrack]] |written= |recorded=[[February 3]], [[2005]]
[[Abbey Road Studios]]{{SW|url=video/endlessly-compelling-the-music-of-episode-iii|text=Endlessly Compelling: The Music of Episode III}} |release date=2005 |represents=[[Duel on Mustafar]][[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']] |performedby=*[[London Symphony Orchestra]] *[[London Voices]] |genre=Film score |length=3:42 |key=D minor, E minor |tempo=𝅘𝅥 = 180 |texture= |timesig=4/4 |meter= |mode= |lyrics= |soundtracks= |movies=[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|III]], [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|VIII]] |EU= |canon= |media={{Audio|BattleOfTheHeroes.flac|"Battle of the Heroes"}}}} {{Quote|Once again, John [Williams] has exceeded my expectations and produced a lavish, rich, moving and thrilling score. Every fan of Star Wars—and of great music—is in his debt.|[[George Lucas]] about John Williams' score for ''[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|The Phantom Menace]]''|{{WebCite|url=http://www.ultimateeditionsoundtrack.com/message.htm|text=The Ultimate Edition -- Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|archivedate=20010713115622}}}} "'''Battle of the Heroes'''" is a musical piece and principal theme composed by [[John Williams]] for [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. The original recording was performed by the London Symphony Orchestra and conducted by Williams. ==Overview== For every new ''Star Wars'' movie, [[George Lucas]] wanted a new major theme. For [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], Lucas wanted John Williams to write a piece that depicted a tragic duel between two former friends. The use of intense choral arrangements echoes Williams's work on [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']], as well as recent film soundtracks like [[Wikipedia:Don Davis|Don Davis's]] work for [[Wikipedia:The Matrix Revolutions|''The Matrix Revolutions'']]. The main theme is at the heart of a climactic performance during "[[Duel on Mustafar|Anakin vs. Obi-Wan]]" and includes musical links to other major Williams's themes: :"Its major recapitulation during 'Anakin vs. Obi-Wan' is agreeable enough, but is offset by a clever musical reference to [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], specifically the climactic Luke/Darth Vader lightsaber fight in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. The allusions to the duality of the story, and to history repeating itself, are unmistakable, and the portentous performances of both [[the Imperial March]] and the [[Force Theme]] during this cue is one of Williams's most welcome touches to the musical heritage of the entire series, and almost rescues the theme from oblivion."{{WebCite|url=http://www.moviemusicuk.us/revengesithcd.htm|text=STAR WARS EPISODE III: REVENGE OF THE SITH|date=2005|archivedate=20081022015000}} SonyBMG released the track as a limited edition CD single and an [[Wikipedia:iTunes|iTunes]] exclusive download, a first for a ''Star Wars'' [[musical score]]. [[Lucasfilm]] created a promotional video for the single release, which was bundled with the CD single and made available through [[StarWars.com]]. The video, a shortened edition of a video available on the [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] ''[[Star Wars: A Musical Journey]]'', included clips from ''Revenge of the Sith''. In the movie, the track starts to play after Obi-Wan and Anakin are caught in a collapsing control arm on [[Mustafar]] and ends when Obi-Wan leaves Anakin on the edge of the lava river. The track was also included on the film soundtrack album released [[May 3]], [[2005]]. A portion of the track plays in the climax of the [[Battle of D'Qar]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VIII ''The Last Jedi'']] as part of the film's "[[Star Wars: The Last Jedi (soundtrack)|Main Title and Escape]]" track. Melodically, the main theme of "Battle of the Heroes" very much resembles the traditional [[Wikipedia:Dies Irae|''Dies Irae'']] melody found in countless works, most notably [[Wikipedia:Hector Berlioz|Berlioz's]] [[Wikipedia:Symphonie Fantastique|''Symphonie Fantastique'']]. This is extremely appropriate, as the text of ''Dies Irae'' invokes apocalyptic images of wrath and judgment. In the [[2005]] ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', "Battle of the Heroes" is played when playing as Obi-Wan Kenobi. In the same game, the cues titled "Anakin vs. Obi-Wan" and "The Boys Continue" are played while playing as Darth Vader. Additionally, it is one of the 4 tracks that can play on the title screen, the others being [[The Imperial March]], [[Duel of the Fates]] and [[The Throne Room|End Title]]. In ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'', "Battle of the Heroes" is a part of the changed music in Episode III levels. A variant of it is also heard in the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' episode "[[Ghosts of Mortis]]," when The Son shows Anakin a vision of what he'll become. The main theme is also heard in the episode "[[Destiny (Star Wars: The Clone Wars)|Destiny]]" when [[Yoda]] begins experiencing visions of the fall of the Jedi. Portions of "Battle of the Heroes" were also heard as part of the Boss battle BMG for the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo DS|Nintendo DS]] version of ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]''. ==Sources== {{IncompleteList|oou=1}} *{{SW|url=video/endlessly-compelling-the-music-of-episode-iii|text=Endlessly Compelling: The Music of Episode III}} *{{SWYouTube|TWTfhyvzTx0|Obi-Wan Kenobi | Teaser Trailer | Disney+}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Themes|saga=1}} {{RWM}} {{Interlang |de=Battle of the Heroes |es=Battle of the Heroes }} [[Category:Themes and leitmotifs]]
